---
title: "A typical jstats workflow"
subtitle: "A full session end to end — from loading data to a finished analysis — with the comfortable, everyday features along the way."
---

::: {.callout-warning title="Skeleton — to be drafted"}
This page has its structure in place; the prose is still to be written. It pulls
the earlier pieces together into one realistic session, and introduces the
everyday "working comfortably" features in the places they naturally come up.
:::

Where the Quick Start raced to a first result and *jstats with your own data*
covered getting files in and out, this page shows what an ordinary working session
actually looks like — start to finish. It runs on the shipped datasets (`community`,
with a `clinic` detour for messy-data steps) so anyone can follow along, and you
can swap in your own data where you like. The package-shipped SPSS/Stata/Excel
copies, reached with base R's `system.file()`, are a natural fit here as a
follow-along source that needs no file of your own.

## Planned arc

- **Open and orient:** load the data, set the working default with `juse`, screen
  it with `jscreen`.
- **Build and clean:** the everyday tidying a real dataset needs — including the
  `clinic` dirty-data beats (declaring user-defined missing values, light recoding).
- **Classify without damage — `jdummy()` registrations:** quick to create;
  non-destructive, fixing a classification without touching the data.
- **Run the analysis:** the core comparisons and models on the prepared data.
- **Control the cases an analysis uses — `jcomplete()`.**
- **Resetting options and clearing registrations** when you want a clean slate.
- **Save and round-trip:** write the results out and reload across formats; the
  `inst/extdata` `system.file()` capstone.
